key_items: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash with RSI mirroring historical 50% rebound setups
Bitcoin bears face $2.6B short squeeze trap as funding rates drop at $60K support
Capital rotation into AI and stronger-than-expected jobs data (172K vs 80K expected) supporting rate hold concerns
reasoning: Bitcoin is testing critical support at $60K with extreme oversold conditions (Fear Index 12, RSI at 2020 levels) that historically precede 30-50% rebounds, while massive short positioning creates squeeze potential. However, macroeconomic headwinds from stronger-than-expected jobs data, AI capital rotation, and regulatory uncertainties (crypto tax hearings, banking capital rules) prevent a strongly bullish setup in the 1-7 day window.
risks: Breakdown below $60K mining cost support combined with macro uncertainty (potential rate decisions, regulatory crackdown) could cascade to $50-55K as Bloomberg's $10K prediction suggests a fundamental loss of institutional confidence.
